steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: Which process powers the Sun?
options: Nuclear fission — heavy uranium nuclei split apart
Nuclear fusion — light hydrogen nuclei join together
Radioactive decay — unstable nuclei emit gamma radiation
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! The Sun is powered by nuclear fusion, not fission or combustion. Hydrogen nuclei fuse together under extreme temperature and pressure to form helium, releasing enormous amounts of energy.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Stars including our Sun are powered by nuclear fusion — hydrogen nuclei fuse to form helium, releasing huge amounts of energy.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.4.5 — Nuclear fission and fusion

steps.title: Nuclear Fission and Fusion
steps.bodyHTML: Nuclear fission is the splitting of a large, unstable nucleus (such as uranium-235 or plutonium-239) into two smaller nuclei, releasing energy and two or three neutrons that can trigger further fissions — a chain reaction. Nuclear fusion is the joining of two light nuclei (such as hydrogen isotopes) to form a heavier nucleus, releasing even more energy per unit mass; it requires extremely high temperatures (∼10⁸ K) to overcome the electrostatic repulsion between the positively charged nuclei. Fission is used in nuclear power stations; fusion powers the stars and is the goal of future clean energy research.
commonErrors: Confusing fission (splitting) with fusion (joining) — remember: fission = split, fusion = join
Thinking fission and fusion are the same process
Forgetting that fusion requires extremely high temperatures to occur

aqa.questionText: Describe how a chain reaction occurs during nuclear fission of uranium-235.

steps.label: Initiate fission
steps.content: A slow-moving neutron is absorbed by a uranium-235 nucleus, making it unstable.

steps.label: Nucleus splits
steps.content: The unstable nucleus splits into two smaller nuclei (fission products) and releases energy.

steps.label: Neutrons released
steps.content: Two or three neutrons are also released during the split.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — two or three neutrons released

steps.label: Chain reaction
steps.content: These neutrons can be absorbed by other uranium-235 nuclei, causing further fissions and releasing more neutrons, creating a self-sustaining chain reaction.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — neutrons trigger further fissions (chain reaction)

aqa.finalAnswer: Neutron absorbed → U-235 splits → releases 2–3 neutrons + energy → neutrons trigger more fissions (chain reaction).

aqa.questionText: Which statement correctly compares nuclear fission and nuclear fusion?

options.label: A
options.text: Fission joins light nuclei together; fusion splits heavy nuclei apart

options.label: B
options.text: Both fission and fusion absorb energy from the surroundings

options.label: C
options.text: Fission splits heavy nuclei; fusion joins light nuclei; both release energy

options.label: D
options.text: Fusion requires no energy input because nuclei naturally attract each other at all distances

markSchemeSteps: Fission = splitting of heavy nuclei; fusion = joining of light nuclei; both release energy ✓
aqa.commonMistake: Option A swaps the definitions — a very common error in exams. Use a memory aid: fission = split (like ‘fissure’ = crack).
